message:
🌱 Add opentofu: Support Concurrent Access to Provider Plugin Cache mission (#830)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "opentofu-1483-support-concurrent-access-to-provider-plugin-cache",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "opentofu: Support Concurrent Access to Provider Plugin Cache",
+    "description": "Support Concurrent Access to Provider Plugin Cache. Requested by 28+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check OpenTofu installation",
+        "description": "Verify your OpenTofu version:\n```bash\ntofu version\n```\nThis feature requires OpenTofu installed locally. Install via `brew install opentofu` or download from https://opentofu.org/docs/intro/install/."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Understand the feature context",
+        "description": "Support the concurrent access/update of provider plugins in the cache enabled by the [Provider Plugin Cache](https://opentofu.org/docs/cli/config/config-file/#provider-plugin-cache)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support Concurrent Access to Provider Plugin Cache",
+        "description": "My initial thought as well was to use a PID (fairly standard), however it may not work well for shared network mounts.  It may not be enough of a downside to prevent this approach.\n\nThe other is to use a timestamp that is updated every few seconds by the locking process as the lock is in place.  If the lock file still exists, but the contents are over N seconds old, it can be invalidated and removed.  We could also record the pid and potentially the machine id in the lockfile for diagnostics,"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the feature is working as expected:\n```bash\ntofu version\ntofu validate\ntofu plan\n```\nConfirm that the feature described in \"Support Concurrent Access to Provider Plugin Cache\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "My initial thought as well was to use a PID (fairly standard), however it may not work well for shared network mounts.  It may not be enough of a downside to prevent this approach.\n\nThe other is to use a timestamp that is updated every few seconds by the locking process as the lock is in place.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "opentofu",
+      "sandbox",

+    ],

+    ],
